{{Quote|Baron-Administrator Calrissian had his own group of Commando-pilots back home. The idea's got merit, even if they couldn't stop Darth Vader from causing trouble.|[[Rhysati Ynr]]|<ref name="XW:RS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>}}
'''Lando's Commandos''' was an elite unit assembled in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] on [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. It was later reformed to rescue several [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]] captured by the [[Eiattu pirates|TIE Pirates]].

==History==
Lando's Commandos was an elite force established by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], the [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. The group unsuccessfully defended Cloud City from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Occupation of Bespin|occupation]].

The Commandos were later reformed in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] from a motley group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]], [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]], and the former Imperial [[Officer/Legends|officer]] [[Von Asch]]. Guided by another former Imperial [[Captain/Legends|captain]] named [[Kine (pirate)|Kine]], Calrissian and his team [[Mission to Radix|assaulted]] the base of the [[Eiattu pirates|TIE Pirates]], and was eventually able to defeat them.<ref name="LC:OEW" />

The unit also participated in the [[liberation of Bespin]] shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="RECG">''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref> The unit was known to use a [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] stealth [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] housed in a [[Museum/Legends|museum]] located on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="LC:OEW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lando's Commandos first appeared in the [[2000]] comic ''[[Lando's Commandos: On Eagles' Wings]]''. They also appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' during the [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] campaign. The campaign was later included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''. The Commandos' appearance in ''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'' takes place prior to their formation in the comic. This article assumes the group was later reformed.
